Description.Name: Bagele
Description.Details:
In the lawless depths of the Circinus, where the strong take what they want and the weak become footnotes, there stomps a legend clad in steel and bad decisions, a pirate known as Bagele "Bagele" Bagele. His story is a cautionary tale of hubris, moonshine, and explosive consequences.

Bagele was born in the grimy underbelly of Circinus, where survival was a full-time job and his parents barely scraped by. As a kid, he learned three things: don't trust anyone, always watch your back, and never, EVER mess with a distille you don't understand.

Of course, he ignored that last one.

One fateful night, drunk on ambition and also actual moonshine, Bagele tried to "improve" a back-alley distille by tweaking its pressure valves. Science happened. Badly. The resulting fireball of liquid stupidity obliterated the distille, half a warehouse, and most of Bagele�s jaw. Waking up in a pirate doc's "clinic" (aka a rusted-out cargo hold with some scalpels), he was gifted a barely functional jaw prosthetic and a newfound appreciation for alcohol-based explosives.

Determined to prove he was more than just a walking OSHA violation, Bagele fell in with a band of pirates who lived by the one rule he could get behind: "If it's not bolted down, it's ours. If it IS bolted down, bring a bigger wrench."

His mech of choice, a Hatchetman, was salvaged from some poor noob who had the misfortune of crossing the wrong pirates. Bagele took to the Hatchetman's up-close brawling style like a fish to water, if the fish had a metal jaw, anger issues, and a bad habit of charging into fights while quoting terrible memes.

Despite his permanently buzzed demeanor, Bagele proved surprisingly effective in a fight. His Hatchetman's cleaving strikes and aggressive, zero-self-preservation tactics made him a nightmare on the battlefield. He charged into combat with the energy of a man who had absolutely drank way too many Four Lokos.

Every pirate raid added to his growing legend, the pirate with the steel jaw and an axe to grind (literally). He became a symbol of reckless chaos, feared by mercs, planetary governments, and anyone unlucky enough to be on the receiving end of a vote from the Pnet high council.
